attestation.statement: Clinician (KL) DATASET-LEVEL clinical sign-off, recorded 2026-07-14. KL directed the authoring (multi-agent + adversarial verification, or curated seed) and attests the records at dataset level, having reviewed representative samples and the methodology — NOT an exhaustive line-by-line review of every record. Clinical sign-off only; regulatory (TGA) sign-off NOT provided; datasets remain -dev and NOT patient-facing. Self-authored/AMH-aligned; corroborate against AMH/TGA before any live use.

reseal_history.reason: R-46: the seal was computed at authoring/ingest time, when incoming records are FORCED to review_status:"draft"/reviewed_by:null; the clinician sign-off (worksheets at eval/pharmacology/signoff/, KL MED0001857758, 88 + 308 records) then set reviewed_by/review_status ON the records and nothing re-sealed. Forensically reconstructed 2026-07-15: each prior seal was reproduced BIT-EXACTLY by reverting only the sign-off, and the clinical content was verified BIT-IDENTICAL to the sealed bytes (provenance stripped and compared) -- the only delta was provenance.reviewed_by + review_status, precisely the fields the sign-off is supposed to change. Nothing was tampered with; no clinical fact drifted; no record re-reviewed or amended. This re-seal blesses records already proven identical to what was sealed. Durable fix landed alongside: contract-pharm-datastore.js now asserts every seal, so CI can never again go green on a broken one.
